ameliyatsiz-varis-tedavisi-nasil-yapilir.title: How to Treat Varicose Veins Without Surgery? Laser, Foam and Radiofrequency Guide
ameliyatsiz-varis-tedavisi-nasil-yapilir.excerpt: How is non-surgical varicose vein treatment applied with laser, foam sclerotherapy and radiofrequency? To whom it is applied, recovery period and options in Trabzon.
ameliyatsiz-varis-tedavisi-nasil-yapilir.imageAlt: Varicose vein treatment without surgery: treatment of leg varicose veins with laser and foam sclerotherapy
body: Non-surgical varicose vein treatment is the closure of insufficient veins in the legs with laser, radiofrequency or foam sclerotherapy (foam treatment) without making a surgical incision. These methods are preferred in the first place in the treatment of varicose veins today; Patient comfort is high and recovery time is short.

What are varicose veins? It is a vascular disease that occurs when the veins in the legs expand and blood leaks back as a result of valve failure. It causes pain, swelling, feeling of heaviness and cosmetic complaints. The goal of non-surgical treatment is to close the problematic vein with heat or medication and direct the blood to healthy veins.

In laser (EVLA) and radiofrequency (RFA) treatment, a thin catheter is inserted into the vein under ultrasound guidance; By applying energy, the vessel wall is heated and contracts. In foam sclerotherapy, sclerosing medication is injected directly into the vein as a foam. All procedures are performed with local anesthesia or light sedation; It is usually discharged on the same day.

To whom does it apply? In patients who have symptomatic varicose veins, who cannot provide adequate relief with medication or compression stockings, or who are cosmetically disturbed, the appropriate method is selected after evaluation with Doppler ultrasound. Laser or radiofrequency is preferred for large main varicose veins, and foam sclerotherapy is preferred for thin and superficial veins.

Compression (varicose) stockings and regular walking are recommended for a while after the procedure. The treatment result is evaluated with control ultrasonography. The advantages of non-surgical varicose vein treatment are: no incision, quick return to daily life, low complication rate and good cosmetic results. These procedures are performed by interventional radiologists in Trabzon and its surroundings.

tiroid-nodulu-tedavisi.title: What is a Thyroid Nodule? Non-Surgical Thyroid Nodule Treatment (Microwave Ablation)
tiroid-nodulu-tedavisi.excerpt: Thyroid nodule definition, symptoms and non-surgical treatment: thyroid nodule reduction with microwave ablation (MTA), to whom it is applied.
tiroid-nodulu-tedavisi.imageAlt: Thyroid nodule treatment without surgery, nodule reduction with microwave ablation
body: Thyroid nodules are lumps or masses that form inside the thyroid gland. Although most are benign, the growth may cause a feeling of pressure, difficulty swallowing or cosmetic complaints. In non-surgical thyroid nodule treatment, the nodule is reduced in size by applying heat via ultrasound-guided microwave ablation (MTA) or laser ablation; No surgical incision is made.

How is thyroid nodule evaluated? First, nodule size, number and characteristics are examined with ultrasound. If necessary, it is confirmed to be benign by fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB). Non-surgical treatment options are considered for benign, symptomatic or large nodules as a result of biopsy.

How is microwave ablation (MTA) done? Under local anesthesia, a fine needle is inserted into the nodule under ultrasound guidance; Microwave or laser energy is applied to ensure controlled destruction of the tissue within the nodule. Procedure time varies depending on nodule size; It is usually discharged on the same day. Afterwards, the nodule shrinks over time and the complaints decrease.

To whom does it apply? In symptomatic or large nodules proven to be benign by biopsy; It is preferred in patients who do not want surgery or who have a high risk of surgery. Thyroid hormones are generally not affected; The procedure targets only the nodule tissue. During follow-up, nodule size is monitored with ultrasound; If necessary, a second session can be applied.

girisimsel-radyoloji-islemleri.title: What is Interventional Radiology? What Procedures Are Done? Biopsy, Drainage, Varicose Veins
girisimsel-radyoloji-islemleri.excerpt: Interventional radiology definition and scope: biopsy, cyst drainage, varicose vein treatment, port insertion. Minimally invasive diagnosis and treatment.
girisimsel-radyoloji-islemleri.imageAlt: Interventional radiology: minimally invasive procedures guided by ultrasound and CT
body: Interventional radiology is the diagnosis and treatment procedures performed with fine needles, catheters or small instruments under the guidance of imaging (ultrasound, computed tomography, MRI or fluoroscopy). Many procedures such as biopsy, drainage, intravascular procedures and tumor ablation are performed minimally invasively without the need for open surgery.

What procedures does interventional radiology cover? Fine needle biopsy (thyroid, breast, liver, lung, kidney, pancreas), cyst and abscess drainage, port (port-a-kat) insertion, varicose vein treatment (laser, radiofrequency, foam sclerotherapy), thyroid nodule ablation, bone and soft tissue biopsies, biliary drainage and stent procedures are within the scope of this field.

What are the advantages? Small intervention area, less pain, short recovery time and most procedures do not require general anesthesia. With imaging guidance, the target is reached with millimetric precision; adjacent tissues are preserved. Many patients are discharged on the same day. In Trabzon, interventional radiology procedures are performed by expert staff.

biyopsi-oncesi-sonrasi.title: Things to Consider Before and After Biopsy | Fine Needle Biopsy Guide
biyopsi-oncesi-sonrasi.excerpt: Fine needle biopsy preparation, post-care and result time. Blood thinners, pain and complications.
biyopsi-oncesi-sonrasi.imageAlt: Patient information before and after fine needle biopsy
body: Biopsy is the process of taking tissue samples from suspicious lesions with a fine needle or thick needle and sending them for pathological examination. When performed under imaging (ultrasound, CT or mammography) guidance, safe transportation to the target is ensured; A definitive diagnosis can be made. Breast, thyroid, liver, lung, kidney and soft tissue biopsies are frequently performed.

Things to consider before biopsy: If you are using blood thinners (aspirin, warfarin, clopidogrel, etc.), be sure to inform the physician; If necessary, medications are stopped temporarily. Specify if there is a history of coagulopathy. Some procedures may require fasting; You will be given specific instructions at your appointment.

After biopsy: Apply pressure to the entry area for a few hours, avoid heavy lifting. Mild pain or bruising is normal; Painkillers can be used. In case of severe pain, bleeding, fever or shortness of breath, contact your healthcare provider immediately. The samples taken are sent to the pathology laboratory; The results are usually available within 3–7 business days. Depending on the results, the treatment plan is determined together with your doctor.

kopuk-skleroterapi-rehberi.title: What is Foam Sclerotherapy (Foam Treatment)? Spider Varicose Veins and Thin Varicose Veins Treatment
kopuk-skleroterapi-rehberi.excerpt: Definition of foam sclerotherapy, spider varicose veins and reticular varicose veins treatment. How many sessions, recovery time and advantages.
kopuk-skleroterapi-rehberi.imageAlt: Spider varicose veins and thin varicose veins treatment with foam sclerotherapy
body: Foam sclerotherapy (foam treatment) is the process of closing the vein by injecting sclerosing medication in foam form into the vein in the treatment of thin varicose veins and spider veins. The drug is mixed with air or gas to form a foam; Thus, wider contact with the vascular wall is achieved, and effective results are obtained with less dose. It can be applied without incision and standing.

Who can use foam treatment? It is used in capillary varicose veins (spider vein), reticular varicose veins and sometimes medium-sized superficial varicose veins. In addition to large veins treated with laser or radiofrequency, it can also be performed as a complementary session for small veins.

How is the procedure done? Local anesthesia is not required; The foam is injected into the vein with a fine needle. Session duration is short. More than one session (usually 1–4) may be required; sessions are scheduled several weeks apart. Compression stockings and walking are recommended after the procedure. Advantages: no incision, short procedure, low cost and good cosmetic result. Foam sclerotherapy is applied by interventional radiologists in Trabzon.

4d-ultrason-bebek.title: What is 4D Ultrasound? When and Why is 4D Ultrasound Performed During Pregnancy?
4d-ultrason-bebek.excerpt: 4D ultrasound definition, the most suitable week in pregnancy (24-32 weeks), baby imaging and safety. There is no radiation.
4d-ultrason-bebek.imageAlt: Imaging the baby's face and movements with 4D ultrasound during pregnancy
body: 4D ultrasound is an imaging method that provides real-time (live) three-dimensional imaging of the baby's face and movements during pregnancy. In addition to classic 2D ultrasound, depth and motion dimension are added; Families can see the baby's facial expressions, hand movements and body position more clearly.

When is 4D ultrasound done? Usually 24–32. It is preferred between weeks (end of the 2nd trimester - beginning of the 3rd trimester) as the baby's face and body can be viewed better. During these weeks, if the amniotic fluid and the baby's position are appropriate, the face and movements are recorded clearly. Diagnostic evaluation is done with 2D ultrasound; The 4D image is for additional information and memory purposes.

Is 4D ultrasound safe? Ultrasound uses sound waves; There are no x-rays. According to scientific data, ultrasound doses used for diagnostic purposes are considered safe. There is no radiation exposure. 4D ultrasound is optional during pregnancy; It offers families the opportunity to see and record the baby. In Trabzon, 4D pregnancy ultrasound is performed by expert radiologists.

port-takilmasi-kemoterapi.title: Port (Port-a-Kat) Insertion: Guide to Chemotherapy and Long-Term IV Access
port-takilmasi-kemoterapi.excerpt: What is a port and who can use it? Chemotherapy port installation, maintenance and duration of use. Infection and thrombosis prevention.
port-takilmasi-kemoterapi.imageAlt: Port (port-a-kat) insertion, subcutaneous vascular access for chemotherapy
body: Port (port-a-kat) is a chamber and catheter system placed under the skin in patients who will receive chemotherapy or long-term intravenous treatment (antibiotics, nutrition). The chamber remains under the skin; Vascular access is provided by entering this chamber with a special needle. Thus, it is not necessary to search for new veins in each session; Patient comfort increases and vascular damage decreases.

Who gets the port? It is preferred for oncology patients who will receive chemotherapy and patients who need long-term intravenous medication or nutrition. Port insertion is usually performed under general anesthesia or sedation, under fluoroscopy or ultrasound guidance. The catheter is advanced into a large vein (subclavian or jugular vein).

Port maintenance and duration of use: Obstruction is prevented with periodic heparin when the port is not in use. When maintained, it can be used for months or years. The insertion site should be kept dry until the skin heals; You can take a normal bath afterwards. It is important to follow sterile use and care instructions to reduce the risk of infection and thrombosis. In Trabzon, port insertion is performed by interventional radiologists.

trabzon-varis-tedavisi-fiyatlari.title: Non-Surgical Varicose Vein Treatment Prices in Trabzon 2025 | Laser, Foam, Radiofrequency
trabzon-varis-tedavisi-fiyatlari.excerpt: How are the prices of non-surgical varicose vein treatment determined in Trabzon? Laser, foam sclerotherapy and radiofrequency fees, insurance and payment options.
trabzon-varis-tedavisi-fiyatlari.imageAlt: Trabzon non-surgical varicose vein treatment prices
body: Prices for non-surgical varicose vein treatment in Trabzon and the Black Sea region vary depending on the method applied (laser, radiofrequency, foam sclerotherapy), the number of veins treated and the price policy of the hospital. One-leg or two-leg, single-vein or multi-vein treatment affects the total cost. In this article, current price ranges and factors affecting the price in Trabzon are summarized.

What is the treatment for varicose veins without surgery? It is the closure of insufficient veins in the legs with laser (EVLA), radiofrequency (RFA) or foam sclerotherapy without making a surgical incision. Procedures are performed under ultrasound guidance in interventional radiology or cardiovascular surgery units. It is usually discharged on the same day; The procedure time varies between 30–90 minutes.

Factors affecting the price: Which method will be used (laser/radiofrequency is generally more costly than foam), how many legs and veins will be treated, whether the hospital is private or public, the materials used and the type of anesthesia affect the price. Prices for single leg vein laser/radiofrequency treatment in private hospitals may vary between a few thousand TL and ten thousand TL; Foam sclerotherapy sessions are generally more affordable.

Insurance and SSI: Non-surgical varicose vein treatment can be covered by many private insurance and SSI contracted institutions. Check with your insurance or Social Security first for coverage; Report and approval conditions vary depending on the institution. Partial or full reimbursement can be made at contracted centers such as Imperial Hospital in Trabzon.

Price information before the appointment: An examination and evaluation with Doppler ultrasound is required for the exact price. After the vascular structure, insufficiency points and appropriate method are determined, a special treatment and price plan is given to you. You can get appointment and price information for non-surgical varicose vein treatment performed by interventional radiologists in Trabzon.

tiroid-nodulu-kac-cm-tehlikeli.title: At How Many Cms Is Thyroid Nodule Dangerous? Size, Biopsy and Follow-up Criteria
tiroid-nodulu-kac-cm-tehlikeli.excerpt: What is the size of the thyroid nodule? When is a biopsy required? 1 cm, 2 cm, 3 cm criteria and follow-up recommendations. Thyroid nodule evaluation in Trabzon.
tiroid-nodulu-kac-cm-tehlikeli.imageAlt: Thyroid nodule size and follow-up criteria
body: Thyroid nodule size alone does not determine whether it is dangerous; Ultrasound appearance (margin pattern, calcification, hypoechoic structure), size and age of the patient are evaluated together. As a general rule, fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) is recommended for suspicious-looking nodules larger than 1 cm or solid nodules larger than 1.5–2 cm. Nodules below 1 cm are often followed; If there is a family history of thyroid cancer or radiation exposure, an earlier biopsy may be considered.

What is a thyroid nodule? They are lumps or masses that form inside the thyroid gland. Most of them are benign and only require follow-up. In a small number of cases, biopsy is performed due to the risk of malignancy. With ultrasound, nodule size (width x length x depth), structure (solid, cystic, mixed), borders and blood supply are evaluated.

Size and biopsy criteria: Risk score is calculated according to TIRADS or similar classifications. Biopsy is recommended for nodules 1 cm and above in highly suspicious appearance, and generally larger than 1.5 cm in moderate suspicion. Complete cystic nodules usually do not require biopsy. If there are pressure symptoms or cosmetic complaints in nodules larger than 4 cm, non-surgical treatment (microwave or laser ablation) option is considered.

Follow-up: Nodules that are benign as a result of biopsy are followed up with ultrasound at 6-12 month intervals. If there is an increase in size or a change in structure, repeat biopsy or non-surgical treatment may be considered. In Trabzon, thyroid nodule evaluation, biopsy and non-surgical treatment are performed in collaboration with interventional radiology and endocrinology.

biyopsi-sonucu-kac-gunde-cikar.title: How Many Days Does It Take to Get Biopsy Results? Pathology and Report Times
biyopsi-sonucu-kac-gunde-cikar.excerpt: Fine needle biopsy and thick needle biopsy result times. How long does it take for the pathology report to be ready? Are results available early?
body: The biopsy result (pathology report) is usually available within 3–7 business days. Routine fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) samples are examined in the cytology laboratory; If special painting or additional inspection is requested, the period may extend to 7–10 business days. Thick needle (core) biopsy and surgical pathology samples may take 5–10 business days as they require tissue (histopathology) examination.

How is a biopsy done? A sample is taken from the lesion with a thin or thick needle under the guidance of imaging (ultrasound, CT or mammography). The samples are placed in formalin or special liquids and sent to the pathology laboratory. There may be slight pain or bruising in the entry area after the procedure; The complication rate is low.

Who announces the result? After the pathology report arrives, the physician who referred you (radiologist, surgeon or endocrinologist) evaluates the result and explains it to you. If necessary, additional treatment or follow-up plans are determined together. If there is an urgent or priority review request, the time may be shortened in some centers; This is specific to the centre.

doppler-ultrason-nedir.title: What is Doppler Ultrasound? Color Doppler in the Diagnosis of Varicose Veins and Veins
doppler-ultrason-nedir.excerpt: Doppler ultrasound definition, color Doppler and varicose vein diagnosis. Doppler examination of leg veins, thyroid and liver.
body: Doppler ultrasound is a type of ultrasound that displays the direction and speed of blood flow with sound waves. While conventional ultrasound shows structure, Doppler adds blood flow; Thus, vascular occlusion, insufficiency or stenosis is evaluated. In color Doppler, the flow direction is coded red-blue; In spectral Doppler, a velocity-time graph is obtained.

Doppler in the diagnosis of varicose veins: Valve insufficiency and reflux in the leg veins are detected with Doppler. Which vessel is insufficiency, the presence of deep vein thrombosis and the treatment plan are determined according to Doppler findings. Before non-surgical varicose vein treatment, color Doppler ultrasound must be performed.

Thyroid and liver: Increased blood supply in thyroid nodules can be seen with Doppler. Conditions such as liver portal vein and hepatic vein flow and renal artery stenosis are also evaluated with Doppler. There is no radiation; It can also be used safely during pregnancy. These examinations are performed in centers providing Doppler ultrasound and interventional radiology services in Trabzon.

skleroterapi-sonrasi-banyo.title: Bath and Daily Life After Foam Sclerotherapy | When to Take a Bath?
skleroterapi-sonrasi-banyo.excerpt: Bath, shower, pool and sea after sclerotherapy (foam treatment). Compression socks and activity recommendations.
body: It is not recommended for the entrance areas to remain wet for the first 24–48 hours after foam sclerotherapy (sclerotherapy) and for prolonged contact with hot water. Short showers can be taken; Generally, 48-72 hours are required for bathing in the bathtub, pool and sea. If your doctor has any special instructions, follow them.

Compression socks: It is recommended to use compression socks for a while after the procedure (usually 1–2 weeks, sometimes longer). You can take a shower by removing your socks; After the shower, the skin is dried and socks are put on again. Socks speed up healing and reduce swelling.

Activity: Heavy sports and standing for long periods are restricted in the first days; Walking is encouraged. Consult your physician for air travel. In Trabzon, follow-up and recommendations after foam sclerotherapy are given in the interventional radiology unit.

lazer-varis-tedavisi-sonrasi.title: Healing Process and Things to Consider After Laser Varicose Vein Treatment
lazer-varis-tedavisi-sonrasi.excerpt: What happens after EVLA (laser) varicose vein treatment? Compression stockings, follow-up ultrasound and return to daily life.
body: After laser varicose vein treatment (EVLA – endovenous laser ablation), most patients are discharged on the same day. There may be mild pain, stiffness or bruising in the leg; It decreases within a few days. The use of compression (varicose) stockings and regular walking are recommended for 1-2 weeks after the procedure; Standing for long periods of time is avoided.

Control: Generally, after 1-3 months, Doppler ultrasound is used to check whether the treated vessel has closed. The vein turns into fibrotic tissue and disappears. Rarely, partial patency or new varicose veins may develop; If necessary, additional sessions are planned.

Complications: Infection, deep vein thrombosis or skin burn are rare. In case of severe pain, excessive swelling in the leg, rash or fever, consult a physician. Follow-up after laser varicose vein treatment in Trabzon is done by interventional radiologists.

radyofrekans-varis-iyilesme.title: Varicose Vein Treatment Recovery Time with Radiofrequency | What Happens After RFA?
radyofrekans-varis-iyilesme.excerpt: Radiofrequency (RFA) varicose vein treatment healing process. Discharge, compression stocking and control.
body: The recovery process after varicose vein treatment with radiofrequency ablation (RFA) is similar to laser treatment. He is discharged on the day of the procedure; Mild tightness or bruising in the leg may last for a few days. Compression stockings and walking are recommended for 1–2 weeks.

The treated vein shrinks within 2-6 months and becomes a fibrotic cord, and no more flow can be seen on ultrasound. The cosmetic result becomes apparent within a few weeks. Control Doppler ultrasound confirms complete closure of the vein.
In Trabzon, radiofrequency varicose vein treatment and follow-up are performed in interventional radiology units.

varis-corabi-nasil-secilir.title: How to Choose Compression Stockings (Compression Stockings)? Pressure Classes and Usage
varis-corabi-nasil-secilir.excerpt: Compression (varicose) stocking pressure classes, size and how to wear. Post-treatment and daily use.
body: Compression stockings (compression stockings) support the upward movement of blood by applying external pressure to the legs. Accelerates recovery after treatment; It reduces symptoms in chronic venous insufficiency. Pressure classes (I–IV) and size (below the knee, thigh, sock) are selected according to need.

Pressure classes: Class I (mild) for daily preventive use, class II (moderate) for frequent use for varicose veins and after treatment. Class III–IV higher pressure; It is used in cases of edema or lymphatic disease with the recommendation of a physician. For measurement, leg circumference is measured early in the morning; The correct size is purchased from a pharmacy or medical store.

Usage: It is worn during the day for the period recommended by the physician after treatment (usually 2-6 weeks); Can be removed at night. Special gloves or sliders can be used to put on the sock. In Trabzon, compression stocking recommendations before and after varicose vein treatment are given under interventional radiology control.

gebelikte-varis.title: What Causes Varicose Veins During Pregnancy? Varicose Vein Treatment and Precautions During Pregnancy
gebelikte-varis.excerpt: Varicose vein formation during pregnancy, treatment timing and postpartum. Compression stockings and safe options.
body: The frequency of varicose veins increases during pregnancy; Hormonal change, pressure of the uterus on the veins and increase in blood volume are factors. Most of them are mild to moderate and partially regress after birth. Invasive varicose vein treatment (laser, foam, radiofrequency) is usually postponed during pregnancy; safety data is limited.

Precautions: Avoiding standing for long periods of time, elevating the legs, walking, and using compression stockings with appropriate pressure (class I–II) may reduce symptoms. If varicose veins continue within 3-6 months after birth, non-surgical treatment is considered.
In Trabzon, varicose vein follow-up during pregnancy and postpartum treatment options are offered in cooperation with interventional radiology and gynecology.

mr-cekim-oncesi-hazirlik.title: Preparation Before MRI: Metal, Pacemaker, Claustrophobia and Contrast
mr-cekim-oncesi-hazirlik.excerpt: What to pay attention to before MRI? Metalware, pacemaker, implant and contrast material. Trabzon MRI preparation.
body: Since MRI (magnetic resonance) uses a strong magnetic field, metal items (jewelry, watches, glasses, underwear clasps, credit cards) should be removed. Pacemakers, cochlear implants, metal clips, and some implants may be contraindicated or conditional for MRI; The form must be filled out before the appointment and the radiologist is informed.

Contrast: In some examinations, gadolinium is given intravenously. Contrast use is limited if kidney function is impaired or during pregnancy. If there is claustrophobia (fear of closed spaces), open MRI or light sedation options may be offered depending on the center.

Fasting: In examinations such as abdominal or MR cholangiography, fasting for 4–6 hours is sometimes required; You will be given specific instructions at your appointment. Preparation and safety information before MRI scan in Trabzon is given in the radiology unit of Imperial Hospital.

bt-bilgisayarli-tomografi-nedir.title: What is CT (Computed Tomography)? Pre-Shooting and Radiation
bt-bilgisayarli-tomografi-nedir.excerpt: CT tomography definition, contrast enhanced CT and low dose. Radiation dose and to whom it is administered.
body: Computed tomography (CT or CT) produces cross-sectional images of the body using X-rays. Since it does not use a magnetic field, CT may be preferred instead of MRI in those with metal implants and pacemakers. Review time is short; It is frequently used in lung, abdomen, bone and vascular evaluation.

Contrast: Iodinated contrast material administered intravenously is used in some CT examinations. Kidney function and allergy history are questioned. Radiation dose varies depending on the type of examination; Low dose protocols are applied when deemed necessary. During pregnancy, CT is postponed if possible or performed while preserving the abdomen.
In Trabzon, CT scans and contrast-enhanced examinations are performed in radiology units.

mamografi-kimlere-yapilir.title: Who gets mammography? Age, Frequency and Screening Recommendations
mamografi-kimlere-yapilir.excerpt: At what age does mammography screening begin? 40 years old, 50 years old and annual mammography. Trabzon mammography.
body: Mammography is the imaging of breast tissue with low-dose X-ray. Screening mammography is used to detect breast cancer at an early stage. According to Turkish and international guidelines, it is recommended to start between the ages of 40-50 and repeat every 1-2 years in women with average risk; It may be done earlier or more frequently if there is a family history or risk factor.

Diagnostic mammography: It is performed without any age limit in the presence of a mass, pain or abnormal finding. During mammography, the breast is compressed; There may be slight discomfort. The result is evaluated by the radiologist; Additional imaging or biopsy is recommended if necessary.
In Trabzon, mammography screening and diagnostic examinations are performed in the radiology department of Imperial Hospital.

ince-igne-biyopsi-agri.title: Is Fine Needle Biopsy Painful? Local Anesthesia and Patient Experience
ince-igne-biyopsi-agri.excerpt: FNAB pain sensation, local anesthesia and procedure time. Thyroid, breast and liver biopsy experience.
body: Fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) causes mild discomfort or pressure in most patients; Complete pain is rare. If local anesthesia is applied to the skin and entry area, the needle entry will be felt less. Since the needle in thyroid biopsy is thin, most patients tolerate it without anesthesia; Local anesthesia is used in sensitive patients.

Processing time: Sampling takes 5–15 minutes. With imaging guidance, the goal is achieved; Several passages (entrance-exit) can be made. There may be slight pain or bruising after the procedure; It is relieved with painkillers. If you are using blood thinners, be sure to inform your doctor.
In Trabzon, fine needle biopsy experience is performed by interventional radiologists, prioritizing patient comfort.

kist-drenaji-nedir.title: What is Cyst Drainage? Percutaneous Cyst Aspiration and Sclerotherapy
kist-drenaji-nedir.excerpt: How is cyst drainage done? Kidney, liver and thyroid cyst. Ultrasound-guided percutaneous drainage.
body: Cyst drainage is the process of draining benign cysts with a fine needle under ultrasound or CT guidance. It can be performed for kidney cysts, liver cysts or large thyroid cysts for symptomatic (pain, pressure) or cosmetic reasons. The needle is inserted into the cyst; The liquid is aspirated. A sclerosing agent (alcohol, etc.) is sometimes injected to reduce recurrence.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; It is usually discharged on the same day. The risk of infection and bleeding is low. If the cyst fluid looks suspicious, it is sent to pathology. These procedures are performed safely in interventional radiology units.
In Trabzon, cyst drainage is performed by interventional radiologists.

abse-drenaji-sonrasi.title: Follow-up and Care After Abscess Drainage | Percutaneous Drainage
abse-drenaji-sonrasi.excerpt: What to do after abscess drainage? Catheter care, antibiotics and control. Trabzon abscess drainage.
body: Abscess drainage is the evacuation of the infection-focused abscess cavity with a needle or catheter under imaging guidance. After the procedure, the drainage catheter can be left in place for a while; It is aspirated daily or drained with a closed system. The antibiotic and painkiller regimen prescribed by the doctor is followed.

Check: Contact immediately if fever, increasing pain or rash occurs. After the catheter is removed, control imaging confirms that the abscess cavity has closed. Liver, lung, intra-abdominal and soft tissue abscesses can be drained percutaneously with interventional radiology.
In Trabzon, abscess drainage and subsequent follow-up are performed in the interventional radiology unit.

4d-ultrason-fiyati.title: 4D Ultrasound Price 2025 | 4D Ultrasound Fee during Pregnancy Trabzon
4d-ultrason-fiyati.excerpt: How much are 4D ultrasound prices? 4D shooting fee and appointment during pregnancy. Trabzon.
body: 4D ultrasound provides live three-dimensional imaging of the baby's face and movements during pregnancy. Prices vary depending on hospital and package; In 2025, average fees for a single session of 4D examination in private centers may range from a few hundred TL to a few thousand TL. Video recording or photography may be available for an additional fee.
Appointment: Usually 24–32. It is done within weeks. Ask when making an appointment for exact price. In Trabzon, 4D pregnancy ultrasound is performed in the radiology department of Imperial Hospital.

tiroid-biyopsi-sonucu.title: How to Interpret Thyroid Biopsy Results? Bethesda Classification
tiroid-biyopsi-sonucu.excerpt: Thyroid FNAB results, Bethesda categories and what to do? Benign, atypia, suspicious and malignant.
body: Thyroid fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) results are categorized using the Bethesda system. Category I inadequate sample; Repeat biopsy may be required. Category II benign; follow-up is recommended. Category III atypia or indeterminate; Repeat biopsy or molecular testing is considered. Category IV–V suspicious; Surgery or lobectomy is discussed. Category VI malignant; surgery is planned.

Your physician (endocrinologist or surgeon) explains the result to you and offers treatment options. In benign results, nodule follow-up is done with ultrasound at 6-12 month intervals. In Trabzon, thyroid biopsy results and follow-up are evaluated in collaboration with endocrinology and interventional radiology.

meme-biyopsi-turleri.title: Breast Biopsy Types: Fine Needle, Vacuum (VAB) and Stereotactic Biopsy
meme-biyopsi-turleri.excerpt: Breast biopsy types, ultrasound and mammography guided biopsy. Which is which in which case?
body: Breast biopsy is the taking of samples from suspicious masses or microcalcifications for diagnosis. Ultrasound-guided fine needle biopsy (FNAB) is used for visible masses. Vacuum-assisted biopsy (VAB) takes a larger tissue sample; Benign result reliability is high. Stereotactic biopsy (mammography-guided) is performed for microcalcifications seen on mammography.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; It is usually discharged on the same day. The results appear within 3–7 days. Surgery or follow-up is planned according to the pathology result. In Trabzon, breast biopsy is performed in radiology and interventional radiology units.

karaciger-biyopsisi.title: Liver Biopsy: How to Perform, Risks and Outcome
karaciger-biyopsisi.excerpt: Indications for liver biopsy, ultrasound or CT-guided procedure, risks and time to outcome. Trabzon.
body: Liver biopsy is the process of taking a sample from the liver tissue with a fine needle or thick needle and performing pathological examination. It is used to diagnose fatty liver, hepatitis, cirrhosis, tumor or liver diseases of unknown cause. When performed under imaging (ultrasound or computed tomography) guidance, the needle is directed directly to the target lesion; thus ensuring safe and effective sample collection.

How is a liver biopsy done? The patient lies on his back or side; The skin is cleaned and local anesthesia is applied. The needle is advanced into the liver under ultrasound or CT guidance; Tissue samples are taken in several passages. The procedure takes approximately 15–30 minutes. Afterwards, a few hours of observation and rest are recommended; The use of blood thinners is evaluated by the physician before the procedure.

What are the risks? The most common side effect is mild pain or bruising at the insertion site. Bleeding, bile leakage, or infection are rare; The rate of serious complications is low. In the presence of coagulation disorders or ascites, the procedure may be postponed or the transjugular method may be preferred. The result usually comes with a pathology report within 5-10 business days.
In Trabzon, liver biopsy is performed safely in interventional radiology units under ultrasound or CT guidance. You can contact us for appointment and preparation information.

akciger-biyopsisi.title: Types of Lung Biopsy and CT Guided Procedure
akciger-biyopsisi.excerpt: Lung nodule and mass biopsy: fine needle, thick needle and CT-guided percutaneous biopsy. Risks.
body: Lung biopsy is the taking of a tissue sample under imaging guidance from a suspicious nodule or mass in the lung or pleura. CT (computed tomography) guided percutaneous biopsy is the most commonly used method; A cytology or histopathology sample is taken by fine needle aspiration or thick needle (core) biopsy. It is preferred in peripheral lesions that cannot be reached with bronchoscopy.

How is the procedure done? The patient lies in the appropriate position on the CT table; The lesion is marked with thin-section CT and a safe access route is planned. After local anesthesia, the needle is advanced through the skin; With CT control, the target is reached and the sample is taken. After the procedure, a short period of observation and a chest x-ray are performed to check for complications (pneumothorax, etc.).

Risks: Pneumothorax (lung collapse) is the most common complication; Small pneumothorax can be monitored, large ones may require tube drainage. Bleeding and hemoptysis are rare. The result is available within a few days with pathology and, if necessary, molecular examination. In Trabzon, lung biopsy is performed in collaboration with interventional radiology and pulmonology.

safen-ven-yetmezligi.title: What is Saphenous Vein Insufficiency? Greater and Lesser Saphenous
safen-ven-yetmezligi.excerpt: Large and small saphenous vein insufficiency, varicose veins and non-surgical treatment. Doppler diagnosis.
body: Saphenous veins are the main veins of the superficial vein system in the leg. The greater saphenous vein (BSV) extends from the ankle to the groin, and the lesser saphenous vein (KSV) extends from the outer edge of the foot to the back of the knee. When valve insufficiency develops, blood flows down instead of up (reflux); This leads to varicose veins, swelling and pain. Saphenous vein insufficiency is the most common cause of leg varicose veins.

How is the diagnosis made? Reflux and insufficiency points are detected with color Doppler ultrasound; It is measured which vessel has reflux and for how long. This map is necessary for non-surgical treatment (laser, radiofrequency, foam) planning. In cases of greater saphenous insufficiency, laser or radiofrequency ablation may generally be preferred, while foam sclerotherapy may be preferred in the lesser saphenous or side branches.

Non-surgical treatment: With EVLA (laser) or RFA (radiofrequency), the incompetent saphenous vein is closed by heating it through a catheter; With foam sclerotherapy, small veins are injected. In Trabzon, evaluation and treatment of saphenous vein insufficiency is performed by interventional radiologists.

spider-varis-tedavisi.title: Spider Varicose Vein Treatment: Laser and Foam
spider-varis-tedavisi.excerpt: Spider varicose veins and capillary varicose veins treatment: laser and foam sclerotherapy. Number of sessions and result.
body: Spider varicose veins are capillary enlargements that appear as a thin red-purple network on the skin surface. Cosmetics may cause discomfort and sometimes a slight burning sensation. Reticular varicose veins are slightly thicker, blue-green veins. Both types can be treated with laser or foam sclerotherapy; no surgery is required.

Laser treatment: Capillaries are targeted with laser beam on the skin; hemoglobin absorbs light, the vessel wall is destroyed and disappears over time. More than one session (usually 2–4) may be required; sessions are scheduled several weeks apart. Pain may be mild; It is relieved with cooling or cream. Foam sclerotherapy: Sclerosing medication is administered intravenously in foam form with a fine needle; It is effective in spider and reticular varicose veins.

Result: Within a few weeks, the veins fade and disappear; Rarely, temporary brown spotting may occur. Spider varicose vein treatment in Trabzon is offered in cooperation with interventional radiology and dermatology.

varis-ameliyati-mi-lazer-mi.title: Varicose Vein Surgery or Laser? To compare
varis-ameliyati-mi-lazer-mi.excerpt: Comparison of classical varicose vein surgery and laser and radiofrequency. Advantages and to whom?
body: In classical varicose vein surgery (stripping), the incompetent saphenous vein is removed through an incision in the groin or knee; General or spinal anesthesia, hospitalization and a recovery period of several weeks are required. In non-surgical treatment with laser (EVLA) and radiofrequency (RFA), a thin catheter is inserted into the vein and the vein is closed with heat; There is local anesthesia, same-day discharge and short recovery time.

Comparison: In non-surgical methods, there is no incision, less pain and bruising, and return to daily life is within 1-2 days. Recurrence and long-term results are similar to surgery with laser/radiofrequency; International guidelines also recommend laser or RFA first for symptomatic varicose veins. Surgery is preferred in cases where laser/RFA is not suitable (very tortuous vessel, occlusion, etc.).

In Trabzon, varicose vein treatment options are offered by evaluating interventional radiology and cardiovascular surgery together. After Doppler ultrasound, the appropriate method for you is determined by your physician.

kompresyon-corabi-sure.title: How Long Should You Wear Compression Stockings?
kompresyon-corabi-sure.excerpt: Compression socks usage period: after treatment and daily use. According to pressure class.
body: The duration of use of compression (varicose) stockings varies depending on the type of treatment and the doctor's recommendation. After laser or radiofrequency varicose vein treatment, it is generally recommended to wear it continuously during the day for 2-6 weeks; Healing and vascular closure are supported. A similar period of time (1–4 weeks) may be recommended after foam sclerotherapy. Long-term, daily use in chronic venous insufficiency sometimes lasts for years.

Pressure class (I–IV) and size (below the knee, thigh) are selected according to need; Class II is sufficient for most varicose veins and after treatment. The general rule is to put on socks every morning and take them off at night; Can be removed during bath or shower. As the sock wears, the pressure decreases; It may be necessary to renew it every 3–6 months.
In Trabzon, compression stocking recommendations before and after varicose vein treatment are given under interventional radiology control.

tiroid-nodulu-iyi-huylu-kotu-huylu.title: Is Thyroid Nodule Benign or Malignant? Biopsy
tiroid-nodulu-iyi-huylu-kotu-huylu.excerpt: Differentiation of thyroid nodule from benign to malignant. FNAB, Bethesda and non-surgical treatment. Trabzon.
body: The majority of thyroid nodules are benign and only need follow-up; In a small number of cases, fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) is performed due to the risk of malignancy. Ultrasound appearance (hypoechoic, microcalcification, irregular edge, etc.) and size are used in risk assessment; Biopsy is recommended for suspicious or large nodules.

The biopsy result is categorized according to the Bethesda system: Category II benign (follow-up), III atypia/undetermined (repeat biopsy or molecular testing), IV–V equivocal (surgery is discussed), VI malignant (surgery). If there is growth or symptoms of benign nodules, there is an option for non-surgical treatment (microwave or laser ablation).
In Trabzon, thyroid nodule evaluation, FNAB and non-surgical treatment are performed in collaboration with endocrinology and interventional radiology.

mikrodalga-ablasyon-fiyat.title: Microwave Ablation Price and Thyroid Nodule Treatment
mikrodalga-ablasyon-fiyat.excerpt: MTA (microwave ablation) thyroid nodule prices and treatment process. Trabzon 2024.
body: Microwave ablation (MTA) is a method of shrinking benign thyroid nodules with ultrasound-guided heat without surgery. Prices vary depending on the hospital, nodule size, and whether it is a single or double nodule; In private hospitals, fees for a single nodule may range from a few thousand TL to ten thousand TL. Social Security and private insurance coverage varies by institution; It is recommended to inquire before the appointment.

The procedure is performed under local anesthesia and can be discharged on the same day; It may take 20–60 minutes depending on the size of the nodule. The nodule then shrinks within a few months; Followed by control ultrasounds. Thyroid hormones are usually not affected. It is applied in the interventional radiology unit of MTA Imperial Hospital in Trabzon.

girisimsel-radyoloji-trabzon.title: Interventional Radiology Trabzon: Where is it done?
girisimsel-radyoloji-trabzon.excerpt: Interventional radiology centers, varicose veins, biopsy and thyroid treatment in Trabzon. Imperial Hospital.
body: Interventional radiology is the diagnosis and treatment procedures performed with fine needles, catheters or small instruments under the guidance of imaging (ultrasound, CT, MRI, fluoroscopy). In Trabzon, this service is provided in the radiology or interventional units of private and public hospitals. Specialist at Imperial Hospital. Dr. Procedures such as varicose vein treatment, thyroid nodule ablation, biopsy, cyst/abscess drainage and port insertion are performed with Doğukan Atabay.

Varicose vein (laser, radiofrequency, foam), thyroid nodule (microwave ablation), fine needle biopsy (thyroid, breast, liver, kidney, etc.), cyst and abscess drainage, port-a-layer insertion and similar procedures are performed on the same day with discharge or short hospitalization. For an appointment, you can contact the hospital call center or outpatient clinic.
For those who want to receive interventional radiology services in Trabzon and the Black Sea region, Kemerkaya, İller Sk. You can apply to the Imperial Hospital radiology department at 27-29.

ultrason-rehberli-biyopsi.title: Advantages of Ultrasound Guided Biopsy
ultrason-rehberli-biyopsi.excerpt: Why is US-guided biopsy preferred? Thyroid, breast, liver and soft tissue.
body: Ultrasound-guided biopsy allows the target lesion (nodule, mass) to be seen on the screen in real time and the needle to be directed directly to the target. There is no radiation; It is widely used in thyroid, breast, liver, kidney, lymph node and soft tissue biopsy. It may be more practical and faster in moving organs and superficial structures than CT or mammography-guided biopsy.

Advantages: With a snapshot, the needle tip is verified at the target; adjacent structures (vessels, organs) are avoided. The procedure time is short and patient comfort is high. Bleeding or pain is minimal. The result is available within 3–7 days with pathology. In Trabzon, ultrasound-guided biopsy is routinely performed in the interventional radiology unit.

floroskopi-nedir.title: What is Fluoroscopy? Live Imaging with X-Ray
floroskopi-nedir.excerpt: Definition of fluoroscopy, areas of use and radiation. Use in interventional procedures.
body: Fluoroscopy is live (real-time) imaging of the body using X-ray; provides a continuous or intermittent stream of images rather than a single static x-ray. It is used to reach the target and control the instrument position, especially in interventional procedures (catheter placement, stent, angiography, some biopsies). It can also be applied in digestive system (barium examinations) and urinary tract examinations.

Radiation: In fluoroscopy, X-ray is given for a short time or in low doses; The shorter the procedure time and area, the lower the dose. If possible, pregnancy is postponed or the abdomen is preserved. Interventional radiologists work to reduce unnecessary exposure. In Trabzon, fluoroscopy-assisted interventional procedures (port insertion, biliary drainage, etc.) are performed in hospital radiology units.

kontrast-madde-alerjisi.title: Contrast Material Allergy and Precautions
kontrast-madde-alerjisi.excerpt: Iodinated contrast allergy, previously known allergy and premedication. CT and angiography.
body: Iodinated contrast materials are given intravenously in CT (computed tomography) and angiography, allowing the tissues to be seen more clearly. Allergic reactions (urticaria, itching, nausea, rarely anaphylaxis) may develop in a small number of patients. The risk of reaction increases with repeated use in those with a previous history of allergy to contrast; therefore, alternative imaging (non-contrast CT, MRI) or premedication (steroids and antihistamines) is considered.

Precautions: History of allergy should be reported before the appointment. The premedication regimen usually starts 12–13 hours in advance. If kidney function is impaired, the contrast dose is reduced or the procedure is postponed. Emergency response equipment is available during the procedure. In Trabzon, risk assessment before contrast-enhanced examinations is carried out by radiology and the relevant clinic.

bobrek-kisti-drenaji.title: Kidney Cyst Drainage and Sclerotherapy
bobrek-kisti-drenaji.excerpt: Kidney cyst percutaneous drainage and alcohol sclerotherapy. Simple cyst treatment. Trabzon.
body: Kidney cysts are often simple (benign) cysts; When it is large, treatment may be required due to pain, pressure or infection. In percutaneous cyst drainage, a thin needle is inserted into the cyst under ultrasound or CT guidance and the fluid is aspirated. To reduce recurrence, a sclerosing agent (e.g. alcohol) may be injected after the cyst has been drained; This is called sclerotherapy.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; The patient is usually discharged on the same day. The risk of infection and bleeding is low. If the cyst fluid looks suspicious, it is sent to pathology; simple cyst fluid is clear yellow in color. In Trabzon, renal cyst drainage and sclerotherapy are performed in the interventional radiology unit.

tiroid-ince-igne-aspirasyon.title: Thyroid Fine Needle Aspiration Biopsy (FNAB) Details
tiroid-ince-igne-aspirasyon.excerpt: How to perform thyroid FNAB, pain and results. Bethesda and follow up. Trabzon.
body: Thyroid fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB) is the process of taking a cell sample from the thyroid nodule with a fine needle and performing cytological examination. It is used to evaluate whether the nodule is benign or malignant. It is performed under ultrasound guidance; The needle is inserted into the nodule and aspiration is performed with several passages. The procedure takes 5–15 minutes; Most patients do not require local anesthesia, there may be a slight pressure sensation.

The result is reported by Bethesda category; inadequate sample (I), benign (II), atypia (III), suspicious (IV–V), or malignant (VI). Follow-up is planned for benign results, and surgery or additional examination is planned for suspicious/malignant results. In Trabzon, thyroid FNAB is performed in collaboration with interventional radiology and endocrinology.

lenf-nodu-biyopsisi.title: Lymph Node Biopsy: US and CT Guided
lenf-nodu-biyopsisi.excerpt: Indications for lymph node biopsy and imaging-guided procedure. Result time.
body: Lymph node biopsy is the taking of samples from enlarged or suspicious lymph nodes for diagnosis. Neck, armpit, groin or intra-abdominal lymph nodes can be biopsied under ultrasound or CT guidance. It is performed to differentiate between cancer spread, infection or lymphoma. Fine needle aspiration or thick needle (core) biopsy is used; Core biopsy yields more tissue and is suitable for histopathology.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; adjacent vessels are avoided by imaging. The result comes with a pathology report within 3–7 days. In Trabzon, lymph node biopsy is performed in the interventional radiology unit.

kemik-biyopsisi.title: How is Bone Biopsy Done?
kemik-biyopsisi.excerpt: Bone lesion biopsy, CT-guided procedure and pathology. Differentiation between trauma and tumor.
body: Bone biopsy is taking a tissue sample from a suspicious lesion in the bone (tumor, infection, metabolic disease) for diagnosis. CT-guided percutaneous biopsy is frequently used; The needle passes through the bone cortex and reaches the lesion. It is preferred over surgical biopsy, especially in vertebral, pelvis and long bone lesions; Less invasive and faster recovery.

The procedure is performed with sedation or local anesthesia; Careful planning is required in lesions with a high risk of pathological fracture. The results are available within a few days with pathology and, if necessary, microbiology. In Trabzon, bone biopsy is performed in collaboration with interventional radiology and orthopedics/oncology.

yumusak-doku-biyopsisi.title: Soft Tissue Biopsy and Imaging
yumusak-doku-biyopsisi.excerpt: Soft tissue mass biopsy, US and MRI guided. Differentiation between lipoma and sarcoma.
body: Soft tissue biopsy is the taking of samples from subcutaneous, muscle or fatty tissue masses for diagnosis. Ultrasound is used as a guide for superficial masses, while MRI or CT is used as a guide for deep or complex lesions. It is performed to differentiate lipoma, hemangioma, sarcoma or metastasis. Core (thick needle) biopsy is preferred for histopathology.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; It is avoided by imaging adjacent vessels and nerves. The result comes with pathology within 5–10 days. In Trabzon, soft tissue biopsy is performed in the interventional radiology unit.

port-enfeksiyon.title: Port Infection Symptoms and Prevention
port-enfeksiyon.excerpt: Port-a-kat infection symptoms, treatment and sterile handling. Chemotherapy patients.
body: Port (port-a-kat) infection is a bacterial infection of the chamber or catheter under the skin. Symptoms: redness, swelling, pain, fever, chills at the entrance site. Infection can be superficial (skin) or deep (chamber/blood); In case of deep infection, the port may need to be removed. Prevention: sterile technique, hand washing and appropriate dressing are important for each use.

Treatment: Antibiotics and local care for superficial infection; Systemic antibiotics and port removal in deep infection. Patients receiving chemotherapy or long-term intravenous access are given training on port care and signs of infection. In Trabzon, follow-up of patients with ports is carried out in cooperation with oncology and interventional radiology.

port-bakimi-nasil-yapilir.title: How to Perform Port Maintenance? Heparin and Cleaning
port-bakimi-nasil-yapilir.excerpt: Port-a-fold heparin lock, dressing and instructions for use. Care between chemotherapy.
body: Port maintenance includes periodic heparin flushing (lock) and keeping the port area clean to prevent obstruction during non-use periods. Generally, heparin lock is performed every 4 weeks; The doctor may recommend different intervals depending on the frequency of use. Dressing changes are performed under sterile conditions; A waterproof cover can be used until the skin heals.

During use, the chamber is entered with a special needle (Huber needle); After the medication is given, it is filled with heparin and closed. If there is pain, redness or fever in the port area, a physician should be consulted. In Trabzon, care instructions for patients with ports are given by interventional radiology and oncology nursing.

varis-tedavisi-sigorta.title: Varicose Vein Treatment Insurance and SSI Scope
varis-tedavisi-sigorta.excerpt: Non-surgical varicose vein treatment insurance and SSI reimbursement. Contracted hospitals.
body: Non-surgical varicose vein treatment (laser, radiofrequency, foam sclerotherapy) can be covered by many private insurance and SSI contracted hospitals. Coverage and reimbursement rate vary by policy and institution; It is recommended that the report and approval conditions be confirmed in advance with the insurance company or SSI. Some institutions only pay for symptomatic (pain, ulcers, etc.) varicose veins.
Partial or full reimbursement can be made at contracted centers such as Trabzon Imperial Hospital; Price and scope can be clarified by providing insurance/SGK information before the appointment.

skleroterapi-seans-sayisi.title: How Many Sessions Are Sclerotherapy Required?
skleroterapi-seans-sayisi.excerpt: Number of foam sclerotherapy sessions, session interval and result. Spider and thin varicose veins.
body: The number of foam sclerotherapy sessions varies depending on the prevalence of varicose veins and vein thickness. For spider and thin varicose veins, 1-3 sessions may be sufficient for most patients; For more widespread or thicker veins, 3–5 sessions may be required. Sessions are usually scheduled 2–4 weeks apart; The decision for the next session is made by observing the closure of the vein and the healing.

Each session can last 15–30 minutes; You can return to normal activity on the same day. Compression stockings are recommended for several weeks. Foam sclerotherapy session planning in Trabzon is done under the control of interventional radiology.

gebelikte-ultrason-güvenli-mi.title: Is Ultrasound Safe During Pregnancy?
gebelikte-ultrason-güvenli-mi.excerpt: Ultrasound safety, sound wave and radiation during pregnancy. Routine scanning and 4D.
body: Ultrasound creates images using sound waves; It does not contain x-rays or ionizing radiation. Therefore, ultrasound used for diagnostic purposes during pregnancy is considered safe according to current scientific data. During routine pregnancy follow-up, the baby's growth, organs and placenta are evaluated with 2D ultrasound; 4D ultrasound is used to show the face and movements live; it is not diagnostic but additional information.

How often should ultrasound be done? According to the guidelines, at least once during pregnancy between 11 and 14 weeks. week (nuchal translucency) and 18–22. week (detailed anomaly scan) recommended; If there is a risk or complaint, additional examinations may be performed. No adverse effects on the fetus have been demonstrated when duration and dose are kept within reasonable limits. In Trabzon, pregnancy ultrasound is performed safely in cooperation with gynecology and radiology.

doppler-varis-tanisi.title: How to Diagnose Varicose Veins with Doppler?
doppler-varis-tanisi.excerpt: Diagnosis of varicose veins and venous insufficiency with color Doppler ultrasound. Reflux and vascular map.
body: Color Doppler ultrasound is the gold standard in the diagnosis of varicose veins. Leg veins are examined; Valve insufficiency and reflux (blood leaking down) are detected. Which vein is insufficiency, the status of the greater saphenous and lesser saphenous, and the presence of deep vein thrombosis are evaluated. Laser or foam treatment is not planned without this map.

The procedure is painless; It can be done with the patient standing or lying down. If the reflux duration is longer than 0.5 seconds, insufficiency is considered. In Trabzon, varicose vein Doppler examination and treatment planning are performed by interventional radiology.

venoz-yetmezlik-nedir.title: What is Venous Insufficiency? Chronic Venous Insufficiency
venoz-yetmezlik-nedir.excerpt: Chronic venous insufficiency definition, symptoms and treatment. Varicose veins and leg swelling.
body: Venous insufficiency (chronic venous insufficiency) is the deterioration of the valves in the veins, resulting in blood flowing back towards the heart (reflux) and pooling in the legs. Varicose veins manifest themselves with leg pain, swelling, feeling of heaviness, skin discoloration and, in advanced stages, ulcers. Standing for long periods of time, pregnancy, obesity and family history increase the risk.

Diagnosis is made with Doppler ultrasound. Treatment includes compression stockings, lifestyle measures and, if necessary, laser, radiofrequency or foam sclerotherapy. In Trabzon, venous insufficiency evaluation and treatment is performed by interventional radiologists.

derin-ven-trombozu-varis.title: The Relationship Between Deep Vein Thrombosis and Varicose Veins
derin-ven-trombozu-varis.excerpt: Varicose veins after DVT, postthrombotic syndrome and varicose vein treatment. Doppler control.
body: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) is the formation of clots in the deep veins of the legs; Vascular damage and valve deterioration may be permanent after treatment. This leads to the development of postthrombotic syndrome (swelling, pain, varicose veins). Superficial varicose veins are common in patients who have had DVT; Before varicose vein treatment, it should be confirmed with Doppler that the deep veins are open and recanalized.

In the presence of active DVT, superficial varicose vein treatment can be postponed; anticoagulation and compression are at the forefront. Laser or foam treatment can be performed safely in the post-DVT period; Physician's decision and Doppler follow-up are important. In Trabzon, DVT and varicose veins are evaluated together and followed up in cooperation with cardiovascular surgery and interventional radiology.

kapakci-yetmezligi.title: Venous Valve Insufficiency
kapakci-yetmezligi.excerpt: What is venous valve insufficiency, varicose veins and reflux. Diagnosis with Doppler.
body: Venous valves allow blood to flow unidirectionally upward (to the heart); In valve failure, blood flows back by gravity (reflux). This causes vasodilatation, varicose veins and leg complaints. It is most commonly seen in the great and small saphenous veins; Reflux duration and degree of failure are measured with Doppler ultrasound.
Treatment: compression, laser/radiofrequency ablation or foam sclerotherapy. In Trabzon, valve insufficiency and varicose vein treatment is performed by interventional radiology.

evla-lazer-endovenoz.title: EVLA: What is Endovenous Laser Ablation?
evla-lazer-endovenoz.excerpt: EVLA laser varicose vein treatment, vein closure with catheter. Improvement and benefits.
body: EVLA (endovenous laser ablation) is the closure of the incompetent saphenous vein by heating it intravenously with laser energy. A thin laser fiber is inserted into the vein within the catheter; The laser beam destroys the vessel wall, the vessel shrinks and becomes a fibrotic cord. There is no incision, local anesthesia or light sedation is sufficient; He is discharged on the same day.
Advantages: short recovery, low complications, good cosmetic result. Compression stockings and walking are recommended for 1–2 weeks. In Trabzon, EVLA is applied by interventional radiologists.

rfa-radyofrekans-ablasyon.title: RFA Radiofrequency Ablation Varicose Vein Treatment
rfa-radyofrekans-ablasyon.excerpt: Varicose vein treatment with radiofrequency, EVLA-like procedure. Conclusion and follow-up.
body: RFA (radiofrequency ablation), similar to EVLA, closes the incompetent saphenous vein by heating it through the catheter; The difference is that radiofrequency energy is used instead of laser. The vascular wall heats up, contracts and disappears over time. Local anesthesia, same-day discharge and use of compression stockings are the same as EVLA.
Both methods are among the first-line treatment options in international guidelines. In Trabzon, RFA is applied in interventional radiology units.

varis-kozmetik-mi-saglik-mi.title: Are Varicose Veins a Cosmetic or Health Problem?
varis-kozmetik-mi-saglik-mi.excerpt: Varicose veins are both cosmetic and health. Symptom and complication. Indication for treatment.
body: Varicose veins are both a cosmetic discomfort and a medical condition. In mild cases, there may be only an appearance complaint; As it progresses, pain, swelling, feeling of heaviness, itching, skin discoloration and rarely bleeding or ulcers develop. Therefore, treatment is recommended for symptomatic varicose veins; Cosmetic complaints alone may be an indication for treatment.
After evaluation with Doppler, laser, radiofrequency or foam sclerotherapy is planned. Varicose vein treatment in Trabzon is offered by interventional radiology.

bacak-agrisi-varis.title: The Relationship Between Leg Pain and Varicose Veins
bacak-agrisi-varis.excerpt: Is leg pain a sign of varicose veins? Venous pain and treatment. Trabzon.
body: Varicose veins and chronic venous insufficiency can cause pain, tension and a feeling of heaviness in the leg; It increases especially at the end of the day and with standing for long periods of time. Elevation and compression stockings provide temporary relief. Pain may be in one leg or both legs; Venous insufficiency is confirmed with Doppler.

When the vein is closed with treatment (laser, foam, etc.), the complaints decrease significantly. Varicose veins should be evaluated in patients with leg pain. Varicose veins and leg pain are evaluated at the interventional radiology clinic in Trabzon.

gece-kramplari-varis.title: Are Night Cramps a Sign of Varicose Veins?
gece-kramplari-varis.excerpt: Relationship between leg cramps and varicose veins. Venous insufficiency and night cramps.
body: Night leg cramps can be due to many reasons; Venous insufficiency and varicose veins are also among the causes. Blood pooling and accumulation of metabolic waste in the leg may trigger contractions. If varicose veins and cramps occur together, venous insufficiency evaluation with Doppler is recommended.

Varicose vein treatment and compression stockings can reduce cramping. Other causes, such as magnesium or vitamin B deficiency, are also reviewed by the physician. In Trabzon, complaints of varicose veins and cramps are evaluated in collaboration with interventional radiology and internal medicine.

tiroid-nodulu-buyume.title: When is Thyroid Nodule Enlargement Dangerous?
tiroid-nodulu-buyume.excerpt: Thyroid nodule growth follow-up and biopsy. What does size increase mean?
body: Size increase is important in the follow-up of thyroid nodules; Growth or structural change (solidification, hypoechoic area), especially over 1–2 cm, may require repeat biopsy or surgical evaluation. If the nodule grows even after a benign biopsy, FNAB or non-surgical treatment (microwave ablation) is considered again.
Ultrasound follow-up is done at 6-12 month intervals. In Trabzon, thyroid nodule follow-up is carried out by endocrinology and interventional radiology.

tiroid-nodulu-soguk-sicak.title: Thyroid Nodule What is Cold and Hot Nodule?
tiroid-nodulu-soguk-sicak.excerpt: Definition of cold and hot nodule, scintigraphy and risk. FNAB indication.
body: In thyroid scintigraphy, a cold nodule is a nodule that does not retain radioactive material (hypofunctional); The risk of thyroid cancer is higher than with a hot nodule, so biopsy is recommended more frequently. A hot nodule produces excessive hormones and is generally considered benign; Biopsy is planned according to size and pressure symptom. Nowadays, in most centers, ultrasound and FNAB are used first; scintigraphy is specifically requested to evaluate hyperthyroidism or nodule function.
In Trabzon, thyroid nodule evaluation is performed in collaboration with endocrinology and radiology.

tsh-normal-nodul.title: Is TSH Normal Thyroid Nodule?
tsh-normal-nodul.excerpt: Thyroid nodule when TSH is normal. Euthyroid nodule and follow-up.
body: Yes; Thyroid nodule often occurs with normal thyroid hormone (euthyroid). Even if TSH is normal, the decision for biopsy or follow-up is made based on nodule size, ultrasound appearance and risk factors. If TSH is low (hyperthyroidism), there may be a hot nodule; If TSH is high, the relationship between hypothyroidism and Hashimoto's is evaluated.
Nodule follow-up is done with ultrasound for 6–12 months. Thyroid and nodule follow-up is carried out in the endocrinology outpatient clinic in Trabzon.

tiroid-kanseri-biyopsi.title: How is Thyroid Cancer Diagnosed with Biopsy?
tiroid-kanseri-biyopsi.excerpt: Thyroid cancer FNA diagnosis, Bethesda VI and surgery. Trabzon.
body: Thyroid cancer can be diagnosed by fine needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB). A Bethesda category VI (malignant) report is considered sufficient for surgical planning; Category IV–V (doubtful) is also often clarified by surgery or molecular testing. Papillary carcinoma is the most common type and is recognized by typical cytological findings in FNAB.

After surgery, additional treatments such as radioactive iodine and TSH suppression are planned by endocrinology and nuclear medicine. In Trabzon, thyroid cancer diagnosis and follow-up is carried out in cooperation with endocrinology, surgery and interventional radiology.

bethesda-siniflama.title: Bethesda Classification Thyroid Cytology
bethesda-siniflama.excerpt: Bethesda categories I–VI, thyroid FNAB results and what to do.
body: The Bethesda system divides thyroid fine needle aspiration biopsy results into standard categories: I insufficient, II benign, III atypia/undetermined, IV follicular neoplasia/suspicious, V suspected malignancy, VI malignant. In Category I, repeat biopsy is recommended, in II, follow-up, in III, repeat biopsy or molecular testing, in IV–VI, surgery or additional examination is recommended.
Thanks to this classification, physicians and patients interpret the results in a common language. In Trabzon, thyroid FNAB results are evaluated in collaboration with endocrinology and pathology.

meme-ultrason-biyopsi.title: Breast Ultrasound and Biopsy Together?
meme-ultrason-biyopsi.excerpt: Breast ultrasound and biopsy on the same day. US-guided breast biopsy.
body: Breast ultrasound is used for screening and diagnosis of a suspicious mass or dense breast tissue. For masses seen with ultrasound, biopsy can be performed under ultrasound guidance in the same session or in separate appointments; In many centers, ultrasound is planned first and then biopsy; In some centers, both can be done on the same day.

US-guided biopsy can be fine-needle or vacuum-assisted; With local anesthesia, pain is minimal. The result comes with pathology within 3–7 days. In Trabzon, breast ultrasound and biopsy are performed in radiology and interventional radiology units.

stereotaktik-meme-biyopsisi.title: What is Stereotactic Breast Biopsy?
stereotaktik-meme-biyopsisi.excerpt: Mammography-guided stereotactic biopsy. Microcalcification and invisible mass.
body: Stereotactic breast biopsy is a mammography-guided biopsy for lesions (especially microcalcifications) that are visible on mammography but cannot be seen clearly on ultrasound. The patient lies face down on a special table; The breast is compressed and the needle is directed to the target by calculating the coordinates with mammography. Larger tissue samples can be taken with a vacuum-assisted system.

The procedure is performed with local anesthesia; There may be slight discomfort. The result is available within 5–7 days with pathology. In Trabzon, stereotactic biopsy is performed by radiology in hospitals with mammography units.

vakum-biyopsi.title: Advantages of Vacuum Assisted Biopsy (VAB)
vakum-biyopsi.excerpt: What is VAB biopsy, larger sample and reliability of benign results.
body: Vacuum-assisted biopsy (VAB) allows multiple tissue cylinders to be removed by vacuum with a single needle insertion; More tissue is obtained compared to fine needle biopsy. It is used for breast and sometimes thyroid/liver lesions. Advantages: better histopathology, reduced risk of false negatives with benign outcome, sufficient sample with a single entry.
The procedure is performed under ultrasound or mammography (stereotactic) guidance; Pain is less with local anesthesia. In Trabzon, VAB breast biopsy is performed in radiology units.

patoloji-sonuc-suresi.title: Pathology Result Time Biopsy Report
patoloji-sonuc-suresi.excerpt: How many days does it take for the biopsy pathology report to be issued? Cytology and histopathology.
body: The biopsy result (pathology report) is usually ready within 3–7 business days. Cytology (fine needle aspiration) samples can be reported in less time (3–5 days); histopathology (thick needle, core biopsy) may take 5–10 days. If special staining or molecular examination is requested, the time will be extended. The referring physician (radiologist, surgeon or endocrinologist) explains the result to you and determines the treatment plan together.
In Trabzon, biopsy samples are sent to hospitals or external pathology laboratories; Result time may vary by laboratory.

biyopsi-komplikasyonlari.title: Biopsy Complications and Precautions
biyopsi-komplikasyonlari.excerpt: Biopsy side effects: bleeding, infection, pain. Prevention and follow-up.
body: The most common side effects after biopsy are mild pain, bruising, and tenderness at the entry site; It decreases within a few days. Bleeding and infection are rare; The use of blood thinners should be questioned before the procedure and stopped if necessary. There is a risk of pneumothorax in lung biopsy and bleeding in liver biopsy; These risks are minimized with imaging guidance and an experienced team.

In case of severe pain, fever, excessive bleeding or shortness of breath, seek medical attention immediately. In Trabzon, pre-biopsy risk assessment and post-biopsy follow-up are performed by interventional radiology.

kan-sulandirici-biyopsi-oncesi.title: Should blood thinners be stopped before biopsy?
kan-sulandirici-biyopsi-oncesi.excerpt: Aspirin, warfarin, discontinuation before biopsy. Physician's decision and duration.
body: In patients using blood thinners (aspirin, warfarin, clopidogrel, DOAC, etc.), the medication can be temporarily discontinued with the physician's decision before the biopsy. The duration varies depending on the duration of action of the drug; Aspirin is stopped 5-7 days in advance, warfarin is stopped a few days in advance, and INR is checked. With DOACs, a short weaning period may be sufficient. Risk (thrombosis) and benefit (bleeding) are evaluated by the physician.

When making a biopsy appointment, be sure to inform us of the medications you use; The physician instructs you to stop and start again. In Trabzon, pre-biopsy drug management is carried out in cooperation with interventional radiology and the relevant clinic.

drenaj-sonrasi-takip.title: Post-Drainage Monitoring and Control
drenaj-sonrasi-takip.excerpt: Follow-up, catheter care and control imaging after cyst and abscess drainage.
body: If the drainage catheter is left in place after cyst or abscess drainage, it is aspirated daily or drained with a closed system; When the amount of fluid decreases, the catheter is removed. The antibiotic and painkiller regimen prescribed by the doctor is followed. If fever, increasing pain or redness occurs, seek medical attention immediately.
After the catheter is removed, control ultrasound or CT confirms that the gap is closed. Post-drainage follow-up is performed at the interventional radiology clinic in Trabzon.

abse-antibiyotik.title: Use of Antibiotics After Abscess Drainage
abse-antibiyotik.excerpt: Antibiotics and dressing after percutaneous drainage of abscess. Infection tracking.
body: After abscess drainage, systemic antibiotics are started by the physician based on culture results or empirically; The duration is generally 1–2 weeks. While there is a drainage catheter, follow the dressing and aspiration instructions. If the fever does not decrease or drainage does not decrease, catheter position or a second focus is considered.
In Trabzon, abscess drainage and subsequent antibiotic follow-up are carried out in cooperation with interventional radiology and infectious diseases.

kist-nedir-tedavisi.title: What is a cyst? Simple Cyst Treatment
kist-nedir-tedavisi.excerpt: Cyst definition, simple cyst and when drainage or sclerotherapy.
body: A cyst is a benign formation filled with fluid; It is common in the kidney, liver, thyroid and ovary. Simple cysts contain thin-walled, homogeneous fluid; Generally, follow-up is sufficient. In large and symptomatic cysts, recurrence can be reduced with percutaneous drainage and, if necessary, sclerotherapy (alcohol injection).
The procedure is performed under local anesthesia under ultrasound or CT guidance. In Trabzon, cyst drainage is performed in the interventional radiology unit.

basit-kist-komplike-kist.title: Difference Between Simple Cyst and Complicated Cyst
basit-kist-komplike-kist.excerpt: Simple cyst vs complicated cyst, ultrasound criteria and biopsy indication.
body: On ultrasonography, a simple cyst is thin-walled, anechoic (homogeneous fluid) inside, and shows posterior wall enhancement; follow-up is sufficient. A complicated cyst has septation, thickening, solid component or calcification; In this case, biopsy or surgical evaluation may be required. Bosniak classification is used for renal cysts; In high categories, surgery is recommended due to the risk of malignancy.
In Trabzon, cyst evaluation is performed by radiology and interventional radiology.

bt-kontrastli-ct.title: When is contrast-enhanced CT performed?
bt-kontrastli-ct.excerpt: When is iodine contrast given? Vascular, liver and tumor imaging.
body: Contrast-enhanced CT is a CT examination performed by administering iodinated contrast material intravenously. It is used for better separation of vessels, liver, kidney and tumors; angiography, liver phases, and tumor characterization require contrast-enhanced imaging. Kidney function and allergy history are questioned; If necessary, premedication is given.
Contrast-enhanced CT is routinely performed in radiology units in Trabzon.

bt-radyasyon-dozu.title: CT Radiation Dose and Safety
bt-radyasyon-dozu.excerpt: Radiation dose in CT scan, low dose protocols and ALARA.
body: Since CT uses X-ray, radiation dose is received; The dose varies depending on the type of examination and body area. To reduce unnecessary irradiation, low-dose protocols, narrow cross-sections and only the necessary area exposure are applied (ALARA principle). During pregnancy, CT is postponed if possible or the abdomen is preserved.
The risk of a single CT examination is considered low; The benefit (diagnosis) must outweigh the risk. In Trabzon, CT scans are performed by the radiology department with up-to-date protocols.

mr-kontrendikasyonlar.title: What are the MRI Contraindications?
mr-kontrendikasyonlar.excerpt: Situations where MRI cannot be performed: metal, pacemaker, claustrophobia. Security.
body: Because MRI (magnetic resonance) uses a strong magnetic field, magnetic metals (some clips, implants, foreign objects) and electronic devices (pacemaker, cochlear implant) in the body are contraindicated or considered conditional. MR compatible implants and MR conditional pacemakers can be removed under certain conditions. If there is claustraphobia (fear of closed spaces), there are open MRI or sedation options.
An implant and metal history form must be filled out before the appointment. In Trabzon, MR safety evaluation is carried out by radiology.

mr-pacemaker.title: Can MRI be performed with a pacemaker?
mr-pacemaker.excerpt: MR compatible pacemaker, MR conditional and MRI shooting conditions.
body: Older pacemakers were considered contraindicated for MRI. Nowadays, many new batteries are "MR conditional" and MRI can be performed under certain conditions (Tesla limit, region, programming). Before MRI, contact is made with the cardiology or battery outpatient clinic; The battery is put into MR mode and reprogrammed after shooting.

MRI is not performed on batteries without MR conditional; alternative imaging (CT, ultrasound) is used. In Trabzon, MRI and battery monitoring are carried out in cooperation with cardiology and radiology.

mamografi-yas-siklik.title: Mammography Age and Frequency Recommendations
mamografi-yas-siklik.excerpt: Mammography screening at what age and how often. 40–50 years and 1–2 years.
body: Mammography screening recommendations vary by country; In average-risk women, it is common to start between the ages of 40-50 and relapse every 1-2 years. If there is a family history or genetic risk, earlier and more frequent screening is recommended. Diagnostic mammography (mass, pain) is performed without any age limit.
In Trabzon, mammography screening and diagnostic examinations are performed in the radiology department of Imperial Hospital.

mamografi-agri.title: Is Mammography Painful? Comfortable Shooting Tips
mamografi-agri.excerpt: Pain and relief during mammography. Compression and breathing.
body: Many women experience mild discomfort or pain because the breast is compressed during a mammogram; Shooting takes a short time.
