Since the breasts may be sensitive during the pre-menstrual period, an appointment can be made after the menstrual period, if possible. It helps to follow breathing instructions and relax. The doctor can recommend painkillers if necessary.
In Trabzon, mammography is performed by experienced technicians.

dijital-mamografi.title: Digital Mammography and Its Advantages
dijital-mamografi.excerpt: What is digital mammography, difference between film mammography and CAD.
body: In digital mammography, the image is recorded directly electronically; It is easier to archive, enlarge, and use computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) than film mammography. The radiation dose may be similar or lower. Nowadays, digital mammography is used in most centers.
In Trabzon, digital mammography is available in the radiology department of Imperial Hospital.

tomosentez.title: What is Tomosynthesis (3D Mammography)?
tomosentez.excerpt: 3D mammography, layer imaging with tomosynthesis. Intensive breast and cancer detection.
body: Tomosynthesis (DBT – digital breast tomosynthesis) is a 3D mammography that images breast tissue in thin sections. In conventional 2D mammography, overlapping tissues may obscure the lesion; In tomosynthesis, this effect decreases, and cancer detection improves, especially in dense breasts. The radiation dose may be slightly higher than 2D; A benefit-risk assessment is made.
You can consult the radiology departments for centers performing tomosynthesis in Trabzon.

ultrason-fizik-prensipler.title: Ultrasound Physics Principles in Brief
ultrason-fizik-prensipler.excerpt: How ultrasound works, sound wave and echo based image.
body: Ultrasound sends high-frequency sound waves into the body that the human ear cannot hear; The echoes reflected from the tissues are collected by the probe and converted into images by the computer. Liquids appear dark (anechoic), dense tissues appear bright. There is no radiation; It can be used safely in pregnancy and children. Blood flow is also shown with Doppler.
In Trabzon, ultrasound examinations are performed by radiology and interventional radiology.

elastografi-nedir.title: What is Elastography? Liver Stiffness
elastografi-nedir.excerpt: Tissue stiffness measurement by elastography. Liver fibrosis and steatosis.
body: Elastography is an ultrasound technique that measures the stiffness/elasticity of tissue. It is used in the evaluation of fibrosis (hardening before cirrhosis) and fatty tissue in the liver; Liver stiffness is reported numerically with devices similar to FibroScan. The stage of fibrosis can be estimated without performing a biopsy.
In Trabzon, elastography and liver evaluation are performed in collaboration with radiology and gastroenterology.

fibroscan-karaciger.title: Fatty Liver with FibroScan
fibroscan-karaciger.excerpt: What is FibroScan, liver stiffness and steatosis with CAP.
body: FibroScan is a special device that performs liver elastography; Liver stiffness (fibrosis) and CAP (fatty) are measured by vibration. It is used as a biopsy alternative in the follow-up of nonalcoholic fatty liver and hepatitis C. The procedure is painless and takes a short time.
In Trabzon, FibroScan is available in the gastroenterology or radiology units of some hospitals.

gebelik-ultrason-hafta.title: In Which Weeks of Pregnancy is Ultrasound Performed?
gebelik-ultrason-hafta.excerpt: Pregnancy ultrasound times: 11–14 weeks, 18–22 weeks detailed scan.
body: Routine ultrasound times during pregnancy: 11–14. week (nuchal translucency and early screening), 18–22. week (detailed anomaly scan). If there is a risk or complaint, additional examinations are carried out. 4D ultrasound is usually done at 24–32. It is applied optionally between weeks.
In Trabzon, pregnancy ultrasound is performed in cooperation with gynecology and radiology.

detayli-ultrason-20-hafta.title: Detailed Ultrasound Week 20 (Anomaly Scan)
detayli-ultrason-20-hafta.excerpt: 20th week detailed ultrasound, organ scan and anomaly.
body: 18–22. A detailed ultrasound (anomaly scan) performed between weeks and weeks is an examination in which the baby's organs (heart, brain, kidney, bone, etc.) are systematically examined. Major structural anomalies can be detected during this period. The process may take 30–45 minutes.
In Trabzon, detailed pregnancy ultrasound is performed by gynecology and radiology departments.

fetal-ekokardiyografi.title: What is Fetal Echocardiography?
fetal-ekokardiyografi.excerpt: Baby heart ultrasound, fetal echocardia indications.
body: Fetal echocardiography is a detailed examination of the unborn baby's heart using ultrasound. It is recommended if there is family history, risky pregnancy or suspicion in routine screening. Heart chambers, valves, and great vessels are evaluated.
In Trabzon, fetal echocardia is performed in collaboration with gynecology and pediatric cardiology/radiology.

x-ray-radyasyon.title: X-Ray Radiation Dose
x-ray-radyasyon.excerpt: X-ray radiation dose, chest and bone radiography. Security.
body: X-ray (X-ray) uses low doses of ionizing radiation; The dose for a single chest or bone radiograph is very low. It is important to avoid unnecessary extractions and protect the abdomen during pregnancy. The diagnostic benefit outweighs the risk.
In Trabzon, x-rays are performed in radiology units.

dusuk-doz-akciger-ct.title: Low Dose Lung CT Scan
dusuk-doz-akciger-ct.excerpt: Low-dose lung CT, smoking and risk group screening.
body: Low-dose lung CT scans for lung nodules and early cancer by giving less radiation than normal dose. It may be recommended for screening purposes in risk groups with a heavy smoking history. Since the radiation dose is kept low, long-term risk is minimized.
In Trabzon, low-dose lung CT is evaluated in cooperation with pulmonology and radiology.

koroner-bt-anjiyo.title: What is Coronary CT Angiography?
koroner-bt-anjiyo.excerpt: Coronary artery CT imaging. Cardiovascular occlusion.
body: Coronary CT angiography is the imaging of the heart arteries (coronary arteries) with contrast-enhanced CT. Congestion and calcium score are evaluated; It can be used for screening purposes before invasive angiography. Pulse reduction and beta blockers may be required.
In Trabzon, coronary CT is performed in collaboration with cardiology and radiology.

periferik-anjiyografi.title: Peripheral Angiography Vascular Imaging
periferik-anjiyografi.excerpt: Leg and arm veins angiography. Occlusion and stent.
body: Peripheral angiography is the imaging of the arm and leg arteries by administering contrast via a catheter. Obstruction, stenosis and need for stents are evaluated. It is performed by interventional radiology or cardiovascular surgery.
In Trabzon, peripheral angiography is performed in hospital interventional units.

embolizasyon-nedir.title: What is Embolization? Tumor and Bleeding
embolizasyon-nedir.excerpt: Vascular occlusive embolization. Tumor nutrition and bleeding control.
body: Embolization is the closure of the target vessel by administering an occlusive material (coil, particle, gel) via catheter. It is used to shrink the tumor (bone, liver tumor) or stop bleeding (trauma, postpartum bleeding) by blocking the tumor-feeding vessel. It is done under imaging guidance.
In Trabzon, embolization is performed in cooperation with interventional radiology and related clinics.

tumor-ablasyonu.title: Tumor Ablation: RFA and MWA
tumor-ablasyonu.excerpt: Tumor ablation with radiofrequency and microwave. Liver and kidney.
body: Tumor ablation is the destruction of tumor tissue with heat (RFA – radiofrequency, MWA – microwave) or cold (cryotherapy). It is used as an alternative to surgery in small liver and kidney tumors; The needle or probe is inserted into the tumor under imaging guidance. It is performed with local anesthesia or sedation.
In Trabzon, tumor ablation is evaluated in collaboration with interventional radiology and oncology.

rfa-karaciger-tumor.title: Liver Tumor Treatment with RFA
rfa-karaciger-tumor.excerpt: Liver tumor radiofrequency ablation. Small HCC and metastasis.
body: If the liver tumor (HCC or metastasis) is small and limited in number, it can be treated with RFA. The needle is inserted into the tumor under ultrasound or CT guidance; Tissue is heated and destroyed with radiofrequency energy. It offers an alternative for patients who are not suitable for surgery.
Liver RFA in Trabzon is planned in collaboration with interventional radiology and oncology.

kriyoterapi.title: Cryotherapy (Freezing) Treatment
kriyoterapi.excerpt: Tumor ablation with freezing. Prostate and kidney cryotherapy.
body: Cryotherapy is the freezing and destruction of target tissue with freezing gas (argon). It is used in prostate, kidney and some bone tumors. The needle or probe is inserted into the target; freeze-thaw cycles are applied.
In Trabzon, cryotherapy application is evaluated by the relevant branch and interventional radiology.

vertebroplasti.title: Vertebroplasty and Kyphoplasty Spine
vertebroplasti.excerpt: Spinal fracture cementing. Vertebroplasty and kyphoplasty.
body: Vertebroplasty and kyphoplasty are interventions that reduce pain and provide stability by injecting bone cement in osteoporotic or tumor-induced spinal fractures. The spinal body is entered with a fine needle under fluoroscopic guidance; cement is injected. In kyphoplasty, space is first created with a balloon.
In Trabzon, vertebroplasty/kyphoplasty is performed by interventional radiology or orthopedics.

kyphoplasty.title: What is Kyphoplasty?
kyphoplasty.excerpt: Balloon kyphoplasty, spinal fracture and cement. Vertebroplasty difference.
body: Kyphoplasty is the process of creating a space within the fracture with a balloon and then injecting cement in a spinal fracture; The difference from vertebroplasty is the balloon stage. Spinal height can be partially corrected. It is effective in painful osteoporotic fractures.
In Trabzon, kyphoplasty is performed in interventional radiology or orthopedic units.

nephrostomi.title: Nephrostomy Tube Kidney Drainage
nephrostomi.excerpt: Renal nephrostomy, urinary tract obstruction and percutaneous drainage.
body: Nephrostomy is the removal of urine by placing a thin tube through the skin into the kidney pool (pelvis). It is applied when the kidney is swollen (hydronephrosis) due to urinary tract obstruction, stone or tumor. It is performed under ultrasound or fluoroscopy guidance; The tube may remain in place for a while.
In Trabzon, nephrostomy is performed in collaboration with interventional radiology and urology.

kolanjiografi.title: Cholangiography Bile Duct Imaging
kolanjiografi.excerpt: Bile duct imaging, PTC and drainage. Jaundice and congestion.
body: Cholangiography is the imaging of the bile ducts with contrast material. It can be done percutaneously (PTC) or endoscopically (ERCP). Obstruction, stone or tumor is evaluated; If necessary, a drainage tube or stent is placed.
In Trabzon, cholangiography and biliary tract interventions are performed by interventional radiology and gastroenterology.

ercp.title: What is ERCP? Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography
ercp.excerpt: ERCP bile duct and pancreatic duct. Stone and stent.
body: ERCP is the process of entering the duodenum with an oral endoscope to visualize the bile and pancreatic ducts and, if necessary, to remove stones or place a stent. It is performed by gastroenterology; Anesthesia or sedation is used. It is used in gallstones, obstruction and pancreatic diseases.
In Trabzon, ERCP is applied in gastroenterology units.

t-tüp-drenaj.title: T-Tube Drainage and Bile Duct
t-tüp-drenaj.excerpt: What is T-tube, drainage after biliary tract surgery.
body: T-tube is a drainage tube placed in the bile duct after biliary tract surgery; It is "T" shaped and one arm protrudes from the skin. Bile is temporarily removed; After recovery, the tube is closed and removed. Interventional radiology sometimes provides similar function with percutaneous biliary drainage (PTBD).
In Trabzon, bile duct drainage is managed in collaboration with surgery and interventional radiology.

perkutan-gastrostomi.title: Percutaneous Gastrostomy (PEG) Feeding Tube
perkutan-gastrostomi.excerpt: What is PEG, tube feeding into the stomach. Indication and care.
body: Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) is the provision of nutrition by placing a tube through the skin into the stomach. It is used for long-term nutrition in patients with swallowing difficulties, neurological disease or head and neck cancer. It is performed under endoscopy or imaging guidance; Local anesthesia and sedation are applied.
Tube care and nutrition instructions are given by the physician and dietician. In Trabzon, PEG is performed by gastroenterology or interventional radiology.
